header.stance: 實質利率壓力上升但未引發廣泛風險緊張，前端主導的熊市趨平格局恢復

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開場論點

7月10日美國公債收盤逆轉前一交易日前端利率回落的走勢，恢復政策主導的熊市趨平。2年期殖利率回升至4.21%，10年期回升至4.56%；即使原油下跌且美股上漲，較短天期債券仍表現落後。這波走勢再次確立本週一以貫之的限制性利率核心解讀，但其當下傳導機制已經改變。能源不再是當日的直接驅動力。投資人反而在聯準會將成長與生產力信心，結合抑制通膨的堅定承諾後，調高了近期實質利率門檻。

這波調整的組成仍相當有序。10年期實質殖利率升至2.32%，通膨補償卻僅溫和上升。這項組合顯示必要實質報酬提高且預期政策路徑轉趨堅定，而非市場失去對長期通膨錨定的信心。Fed的半年度評估承認，戰爭、關稅及科技成本已推升通膨，但也指出截尾指標有所改善。由於前瞻指引刻意降低明確程度，市場更加重視即將公布的通膨數據，以及經濟活動穩健可能讓政策制定者維持限制性立場的風險。

長存續期間壓力持續存在，但並未主導當日拋售。30年期殖利率維持在5.06%，延續前一交易日上漲後仍未消退的高融資門檻。日本提供了部分反向力量：官方鼓勵退休基金增加國內投資，推動JGB大幅上漲並帶動日圓走強。全球存續期間的即時壓力因而緩解，然而同一項提議也引發日本對海外證券需求的長期疑問。因此，美國殖利率曲線因政策風險而趨平，國際投資人基礎訊號則朝2個方向發展。

信用與跨資產走勢不支持普遍性融資事件的解讀。高收益債上市工具近乎持平，股市上漲且波動率下降，儘管存續期間較長的投資級債券報酬轉弱。銀行資產負債表成長及私募信貸贖回秩序良好，支持融資的可得性，但異常緊窄的現券利差、精簡的交易商庫存，以及Aston Martin債權人展開動員，都顯示容錯空間有限。收盤呈現總體韌性與選擇性資產負債表謹慎並存：近期Fed壓力增強，長端補償維持高檔，而風險偏好僅在槓桿及再融資需求可控的領域保持完好。

## 美國公債利率、Fed定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美國公債由前端開始依天期遞減走弱。2年期殖利率上升5個基點至4.21%，5年期上升3個基點至4.30%，10年期上升2個基點至4.56%，30年期則微升1個基點至5.06%。這完全逆轉週四2年期債券的漲勢，並使指標債及長債回到週三水準。此一排序顯示政策路徑正在調整：投資人要求在預期緊縮區間獲得更多補償，而長端增幅較小，是因為相當程度的期限與供給溢價早已反映在價格中。

殖利率曲線指標呈現熊市趨平。2s10s利差收窄3個基點至35，2s30s則收窄4個基點至85。2s5s10s蝶式利差下降1個基點至-17，因5年期債券相對2年期仍獲得部分支撐；2s10s30s蝶式利差下滑2個基點至-15，因指標債券區段相對長債走弱。因此，週五逆轉了先前的牛市趨陡，但未造成新的長端主導型財政拋售。

抗通膨債券顯示實質利率處於這波走勢的核心。5年期實質殖利率上升3個基點至2.02%，7年期上升2個基點至2.16%，10年期則上升1個基點至2.32%。20年期及30年期實質殖利率維持在2.66%及2.86%不變。實質殖利率壓力集中於5年至10年期區段，與名目殖利率曲線相呼應，進一步支持市場對政策持續性的重新定價，強於對遠期通膨或財政風險重新定價的解讀。

10年期損益兩平通膨率上升1個基點至2.24%，名目與實質部分對指標殖利率上升2個基點的貢獻相同。若看1個月期間，對比更為鮮明：10年期實質殖利率上升12個基點，損益兩平通膨率則下降9個基點。週五中斷近期通膨補償下降的走勢，但未扭轉較廣泛的格局；即使市場隱含通膨回落，投資人對承擔存續期間所要求的實質報酬仍然提高。

單週變化顯示較長天期的結構性負擔仍在。2年、5年及10年期名目殖利率均較1週前上升7個基點，30年期則上升8個基點。若看1個月期間，2年期上升8個基點，長債上升5個基點，相較之下5年期及10年期分別上升4個及3個基點。當日殖利率曲線趨平，發生於整條曲線殖利率長期處於高檔的格局之中；無論政策區段或最長端，均未能守住週四的漲勢。

聯準會半年度《貨幣政策報告》提供了當日最明確的政策訊號。報告將成長描述為穩健、生產力強勁，並認為銀行體系具有韌性。過去1年，伊朗戰爭、關稅及科技產品成本的影響使通膨上升，但截尾指標已有緩和。政策制定者在這項好壞參半的診斷之外，亦表明若長期通膨預期受到威脅，準備採取強力行動，使利率市場缺乏理由將原油走軟視為足夠的政策寬鬆條件。

前瞻指引減少，提升了每項後續數據的重要性。匯率選擇權已開始反映下一批美國通膨數據公布前後的事件風險上升，貨幣市場也計入年內至少升息25個基點。報告對預測不確定性的強調，支持依數據變化的機率分布，而非預設的政策次序。週五前端走弱正反映這種不對稱性：成長強勁或通膨持續可印證進一步緊縮，市場若要獲得緩解，則需通膨與需求的多項組成同步轉弱。

商品訊號限制了鷹派解讀。WTI及Brent當日均下跌，10年期損益兩平通膨率僅小幅變動。然而，在脆弱的停火協議後，成品油並未跟隨原油下跌，煉油產量受限及柴油供應中斷使裂解價差維持高檔。USDA的7月供需平衡表另增1項食品價格尾端風險，下調美國小麥產量至15.36億英斗，並調降玉米及全球穀物庫存。這2項管道均未造成損益兩平通膨率失控上升，但都限制了市場對整體通膨持續降溫的信心。

國庫券殖利率曲線也轉向反映更堅定的政策路徑。4週期票息等值殖利率維持在3.69%不變，6週期及8週期殖利率則上升1個基點至3.69%及3.72%。13週期及17週期殖利率上升2個基點至3.80%及3.88%，26週期及52週期區段則上升4個基點至3.96%及4.06%。2個月以外天期的升幅逐步加大，顯示現金工具主要重新定價預期政策，而非即時融資壓力。

長端走勢延續本週的供給結論，但標售承接能力未進一步惡化。30年期名目殖利率維持在5%以上，實質殖利率保持在2.86%，儘管長債表現優於較短天期債券。目前定價證實，投資人仍要求對財政借款及企業債存續期間競爭給予可觀補償。同時，當日殖利率曲線趨平且長期實質殖利率持穩，並未顯示長期通膨或需求受到額外衝擊。

因此，這次利率收盤傳達的限制性訊號，比先前由油價驅動的拋售更為集中。Fed溝通及經濟活動展現韌性，推高近期實質利率門檻；損益兩平通膨率溫和走高，使通膨尾端風險仍在；原油下跌則防止該風險成為主導因素。長端殖利率居高不下，維持供給與期限溢價的主題，但當日相對走勢由前端主導。若要形成持久變化，現在需要通膨數據改變反應函數，而非整體能源價格再次波動。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

美國公債殖利率上升之際，上市信用資產走軟，品質區隔主要可由存續期間解釋。LQD下跌0.23%至$107.46，HYG小跌0.05%至$79.71，JNK則下跌0.07%至$95.93。高收益債變動不大，加上股市走強且波動率下降，顯示企業風險並未普遍遭到拋售。投資級債券總報酬較容易受到基準利率上升影響，因此現券估值、交易商承接能力及個別發行人的融資需求，更能反映信用市場韌性。

現券利差在收盤前幾乎沒有緩衝。企業債券本週大部分時間的殖利率約較美國公債高74個基點，接近數十年低點。截至6月止的年內期間，初級交易商平均淨放空約$40億企業債，是可追溯至1998年的數據中首次出現此種部位。長天期部位驅動了這項立場：交易商放空約$137億5年後到期的債券，部分被較短天期債券$96.6億的多頭部位抵銷。

這種庫存型態兼具總體與結構性原因。當利差受到壓縮時，較高利率與地緣政治不確定性使長天期企業債不適合留在庫存。電子交易目前占投資級債券交易的49%及高收益債交易的32%，讓交易商能以較少的資產負債表庫存撮合買賣流量。退休基金及保險公司的強勁需求，也可能使交易商帳面上幾乎沒有存貨。結果是正常環境下市場具備流動性，但若殖利率或利差突然變動，可見的中介承接能力將會降低。

歐洲估值呈現基本面強勁與補償有限之間的相同張力。投資級債利差在回吐伊朗戰爭期間的擴大幅度後，已接近全球金融危機以來最窄水準。RBC Wealth Management認為歐元計價債券尤其昂貴，美元與英鎊市場則具有較佳的相對價值，同時仍承認企業資產負債表穩健。其疑慮在於估值與存續期間曝險，而非借款人品質即將惡化。

Lendable 規模 5 億英鎊的個人貸款證券化交易，顯示投資人仍願意承接較短天期、收益較高的資產。該平台已承作逾 100 億英鎊消費信貸資產，2025 年營收達 4.46 億英鎊、成長 90%，營業利益為 1.14 億英鎊。這筆交易支援其在英國、美國及墨西哥擴大放款，同時把消費者信用風險轉移至資產擔保證券。相較長天期投資級現券，較短且逐步攤還的資產可提供利差與本金回收；但無擔保家庭信貸仍對就業與再融資環境敏感。

MiniMax 則以混合工具為人工智慧投資籌資。這家中國模型開發商計畫以每股 HK$268 的固定價格發行 3,560 萬股、募集 HK$9.5 billion，較收盤價折價 9.9%；同時發行 2027 年到期、規模 HK$6.5 billion 的零息可轉債，轉換溢價為 25%。整體募資額接近 $2 billion，將融資風險分散至立即的股權稀釋與未來轉換。這也把人工智慧資本需求由美國超大規模雲端業者債券延伸至其他市場：估值許可時，股權與可轉債可降低現金利息，但股價盤中下跌近 9.8%，顯示稀釋與執行仍有代價。

商業銀行數據支持融資可得性，但顯示企業信貸動能放緩。6月銀行信貸按年率計成長4.7%，放款與租賃則成長5.2%。商業與工業貸款增加3.7%，遠低於5月的10.9%及4月的15.9%。存款成長5.3%，其中其他存款增加7.6%，大額定期存款則萎縮10%。這些數據顯示放款管道仍在運作，但對企業活動的邊際推力正在降溫。

H.8報告於美東時間下午4:15公布，因此與收盤最後階段相關，但無法解釋稍早的利率走勢。截至7月1日止當週，銀行信貸為$19.6151兆，C&I貸款為$2.8781兆，存款則為$19.4345兆，三者均略低於前1週水準。月度擴張與週度走軟相互吻合，呈現融資環境仍能正常運作，但對槓桿型企業活動提供的加速作用已減弱。

Fed的金融穩定評估也劃出相似界線。家庭與企業總債務相對GDP的比率降至2000年代初期以來最低，銀行資本維持高檔，未受保險存款自2023年初以來也有所下降。然而，避險基金槓桿仍接近歷史高點，並集中於少數機構。部分私募信貸工具面臨增加的贖回要求，市場維持正常運作時，大致可滿足最高達資產淨值5%的贖回需求。流動性仍然可得，但槓桿與贖回條款依舊可能放大衝擊。

Aston Martin說明了有利的整體背景如何與嚴重的資本結構風險並存。Arini Capital Management、BlackRock及Sculptor率領持有該車廠逾半數優先擔保票據的團體，提出新融資，同時試圖防止任何可能使既有債權受償順位下降的交易。第1季稅前虧損為£6,550萬，淨負債達£14.6億，另有等值$18.5億債券將於2029年到期。這份合作協議使討論焦點從整體市場准入，轉向受償順位、契約文件及回收價值。

Shapoorji Pallonji 位於市場融資順暢與困境重整之間。這家印度集團在 Goswami Infratech 債務 2 度展期後，啟動規模 2,550 億盧比、約 $27 億的再融資計畫。其中約 1,500 億盧比本幣債券配售給 Farallon、Davidson Kempner 與 Cerberus 等投資人，另有 $6.5 億、3 年期美元債以 14.5% 殖利率完成；Deutsche Bank 可能投資約 $4 億。這套融資恢復近期流動性，但雙位數美元殖利率與對少數機構資金的依賴，顯示融資管道仍然昂貴；該集團持有的 Tata Sons 18.4% 股權遲未變現，也使去槓桿進度更加複雜。

因此，信用市場仍然開放，但保護緩衝偏薄。投資人需求及科技支援的交易方式維持次級市場流動性，銀行持續擴張信貸，私募市場也消化了較高的贖回需求。利差偏窄降低了對總體意外的補償，交易商空頭部位使風險集中於較長天期，而財務受壓的借款人則在到期壓力迫近前，就需要債權人展開協調。週五基金小幅虧損與市場正常運作並不矛盾，但基準殖利率高企且利差狹窄的組合，使融資結果愈來愈取決於結構及發行人的現金創造能力。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

日本對美國公債拋售提供了已開發市場中最強的反向力量。財務大臣Satsuki Katayama敦促退休基金，包括政府年金投資基金，增加對國內金融資產的投資。這番言論推動20年期JGB殖利率下跌12個基點至3.745%，10年期殖利率下跌11.5個基點至2.76%，日圓曾上漲0.7%。這波漲勢在亞洲交易時段降低了全球存續期間的即時壓力，也顯示投資人基礎政策可在單日壓過本週的通膨疑慮。

跨境影響並非一致有利。GPIF管理¥293.6兆資產，約有一半投資於海外；日本也持有約$1.2兆美國公債。若持久轉向國內債券及股票，可能穩定陡峭的JGB殖利率曲線並減少結構性日圓賣壓，卻也可能縮小配置於外國存續期間資產的資金池。當下JGB上漲有利全球債券，但配置轉移若完成，美國公債需求可能對相對殖利率及匯率避險成本變得更敏感。

執行方式仍不確定。GPIF由勞動省監督，遵循5年期策略性資產配置流程，並在2025年3月檢討時，維持國內債券、國內股票、外國債券及外國股票各占25%的配置。Katayama的言論因此先改變市場預期，尚未改變正式授權。這波漲勢緩解了財政與BOJ政策正常化壓力，但仍未解答公共退休基金是否會承接更多國內發債，或政府是否只是運用口頭指引，緩解日圓及長天期JGB的壓力。

日本生產者價格強化了資產配置辯論中的通膨面向。BOJ 企業物價指數 6 月年增 7.1%，為 2023 年初以來最快，較 5 月則上升 0.4%，前月數據也向上修正。加上年度薪資談判連續第3年加薪逾 5%，結果顯示企業正把更多投入成本轉嫁給客戶，而非完全由利潤率吸收。退休基金需求可以穩定 JGB 存續期間，但當企業定價、信貸成長與薪資都偏強時，無法消除政策正常化的理由。

德國通膨帶來雙向的歐洲存續期間訊號。6月CPI確認較上年同期上升2.3%，較5月下跌0.3%；5月年增率為2.6%。HICP較上年同期上升2.4%，較上月下跌0.2%。能源價格單月下降3.0%，其中汽車燃料及取暖用油大幅下跌，為德國公債提供近期通膨降溫管道。核心通膨維持在2.5%，服務價格上升3.1%，使ECB仍受基礎價格壓力約束。

加拿大就業報告較明確地不利於近期存續期間資產獲得支撐，但內容並非全面強勁。就業人數在5月增加88,000後再增18,000，就業率升至60.8%，失業率則降至6.5%。平均時薪年增率由3.0%加速至3.3%。製造業就業減少17,000，顯示產業層面的疲弱，但薪資更加堅挺且失業率下降，使加拿大央行的通膨與成長權衡仍偏限制性。

ECB管理委員會成員Emmanuel Moulin提出另一項需要審慎的結構性理由。他認為，人工智慧可透過資本支出在短期內推高通膨，之後再藉由生產力降低通膨；這會提高價格路徑的波動，但目前尚不致改變現行政策。這項訊息呼應Fed自身對AI的看法，即AI既是生產力來源，也是科技產品需求的驅動因素。各地的大型投資計畫正在支撐經濟活動，同時讓通膨降溫的時點更加複雜。

全球存續期間資產以分歧而非同步拋售結束當日交易。日本潛在的國內買盤支持使JGB殖利率大幅下降，德國能源通膨降溫緩和整體訊號，加拿大薪資則維持了政策緊縮的可信度。對美國公債而言，這些力量分別帶來即時利率緩解及長期資本競爭。週五美國公債即使在JGB上漲時仍可出現熊市趨平，是因為美國市場的主導催化劑為Fed政策路徑；30年期殖利率持續高企，則反映財政供給及跨境資產配置風險仍未解決。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

儘管美國公債殖利率上升，新興市場美元債仍保持穩定。EMB上漲0.03%至$95.99，美元指數幾乎持平，小升0.03%至100.97。即使美國實質殖利率上升，此一組合仍使硬通貨借款人免受額外美元衝擊。因此整體表現看似穩定，但主權債結果仍分為獲得寬鬆空間的國家，以及面臨市場准入受損的國家。

巴西的本國存續期間資產獲得最明確支撐。6月年通膨率放緩至4.64%，低於調查中的所有預估，預估中位數為4.80%；月通膨率則降至0.16%，是10月以來最低。食品與飲料價格下跌0.24%，抵銷住房價格上升0.63%的影響。這項低於預期的結果緊接在5月就業創造疲弱之後，增強了8月5日政策會議在連續3次降息後，再降息25個基點的可能性。

財政政策限制了寬鬆幅度。Selic利率目前為14.25%，央行預測仍顯示2026年底通膨將高於目標區間上限。家庭債務協商支持、補貼信貸，以及部分燃料稅暫時停徵，都在選舉接近之際增加需求。當前通膨放緩可延續漸進式寬鬆，但服務、住房通膨或財政刺激若持續，將使實質利率維持高檔，並限制巴西本國債務的重新定價幅度。

塞內加爾則處於債務管理光譜的另一端。審計揭露約$70億先前未申報的貸款，後續檢討使公共債務估值從原先的GDP占比74%，上調至逾130%。IMF暫停1項$18億計畫，信評進一步降入垃圾級，該國實際上失去了進入歐洲債券市場的管道。其國際債券相對美國公債的平均利差超過15個百分點，反映市場預期延長到期日或債務重組可能已無法避免。

融資壓力不只影響主權債券持有人。塞內加爾今年面臨約5.5兆CFA francs債務償付，相較之下預期稅收約為5.4兆francs。該國更加依賴西非區域市場，使銀行增加對短期政府國庫券的曝險，將主權風險傳導至當地資產負債表。以歐元及美元融資的專案，收入卻以CFA francs計價，形成國內現金流難以彌補的貨幣錯配。官方信譽及外匯創造能力的重要性，如今已超越全球風險偏好。

因此，新興市場在指數層次保持平靜，內部差異卻十分顯著。美元持穩且上市債務展現韌性，降低了共同的再融資衝擊。巴西通膨低於預期，提高了在財政限制下溫和實施本國寬鬆的可能性。塞內加爾的隱藏負債、官方融資暫停及區域市場短期借款，顯示揭露品質不佳如何使現任政府無法進入硬通貨市場。美國實質殖利率上升仍是1項障礙，但國內政策信譽決定了這只是邊際壓力，還是生存性約束。

## 跨資產

美股將週五的總體組合視為與持續成長相容。S&P 500上漲0.42%至7,575.39，Nasdaq 100上漲0.33%至29,825.11，費城半導體指數則上漲0.06%至12,967.16。VIX下跌5.11%至15.03。美國公債殖利率上升之際，股市走強且波動率下降，支持債券拋售源於實質利率與政策的解讀，幾乎沒有跡象顯示投資人正在尋求保護，以防經濟成長即將中斷。

能源與利率呈反向走勢。WTI下跌0.93%至$71.41，Brent下跌0.38%至$76.01。兩者較1週前仍分別上漲3.96%及5.86%，但1個月跌幅依然顯著。當日油價下跌緩和了先前幾個交易日的直接整體通膨動能。成品油利潤仍高，加上地緣政治摩擦再起，使傳導風險持續存在，也解釋了為何原油本身回落時，損益兩平通膨率仍微幅走高。

美元幾乎持平於100.97，美國殖利率上升並未得到強力匯市訊號的確認。儘管地緣政治仍有不確定性，黃金仍下跌0.64%至$4,104.10。實質殖利率上升提高了持有黃金的機會成本，美元未大幅變動則使跌勢未演變為廣泛的避險資產平倉。黃金走弱與實質利率重新定價更為一致，而非反映當日股市的有利氛圍。

低匯率波動率仍是支撐風險承擔的重要因素，但即將公布的美國通膨數據已推高避險成本。全球利率差距擴大且匯率平穩，已協助G10套利策略在年內取得約8%的報酬。Fed減少前瞻指引，使這種穩定更依賴每次數據公布。波動率若急升，可能迫使槓桿套利部位平倉，並透過美元、新興市場融資成本及跨境存續期間需求，傳導美國政策意外。

因此，跨資產收盤使債券走勢更為複雜，但並未與之矛盾。油價下跌、股市上漲且波動率下降，否定了廣泛通膨型避險行情的解讀。黃金走弱及前端主導的美國公債拋售，指向實質折現率上升；美元持平且高收益債展現韌性，則顯示金融情勢並未無序收緊。市場接受政策限制增強與成長持續並存，使未來衝擊中的脆弱點，落在槓桿套利、緊窄信用利差及高實質殖利率的交會處。

## 市場前瞻意義

7月14日美東時間上午8:30公布的6月CPI及實質所得，將開啟關鍵的反應函數數據序列，接著是主席Kevin Warsh於上午10:00出席House聽證；7月15日上午8:30公布6月PPI，上午10:00則出席Senate聽證。若核心及服務價格全面降溫、實質所得改善、生產者價格轉嫁有限，且Fed承認政策可維持耐心，將挑戰週五由前端主導的反轉，並拉低2年至5年期實質殖利率。基礎通膨堅挺、實質所得成長疲弱，或明確強調進一步緊縮，則會證實原油下跌尚未降低政策緊迫性。2年期殖利率、短期通膨補償、美元及事件波動率是最敏感的傳導管道，因為目前論點取決於限制性Fed政策路徑與長期通膨預期受控並存。

7月13日美東時間上午11:30，美國財政部將標售$920億13週期國庫券及$790億26週期國庫券，接著於7月14日同一時間標售$950億42天期國庫券；3筆國庫券均於7月16日結算。投標倍數強勁、直接及間接投資人廣泛參與、交易商得標比例有限，加上國庫券次級市場交易穩定，將部分化解大量現金承接可能擾動原本健康融資環境的疑慮。投標疲弱、交易商保留更多部位，或結算前再度走低，將重新引發這項風險，尤其週五較長天期國庫券殖利率的升幅已高於最短天期。國庫券殖利率曲線、擔保融資環境及對銀行流動性敏感的信用資產，是相關傳導管道。承接有序將證實熊市趨平反映政策預期，需求不佳則會為前端增添現金管理溢價。

GPIF或日本勞動省對增加國內投資呼籲的正式回應並無固定日期，但啟動任何資產配置檢討、變更目標區間，或向其他公共退休基金提供指引，都將構成可觀察的觸發條件。若可信地轉向國內債券，使超長天期JGB殖利率持續下降且日圓走強，將證實日本可透過投資人基礎穩定自身殖利率曲線，同時也會引發日本對美國公債需求減少的疑問。若官方否認、程序延誤，或週五JGB漲勢迅速逆轉，將挑戰即時緩解的論點，並重新引發財政與BOJ政策正常化壓力。超長天期JGB、日圓、匯率避險後的美國公債報酬及美國長端，將顯示這項政策訊號帶來的是持久的國內承接，還是僅有暫時性的口頭干預效果。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有註明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為準。

overall_summary.headline: Fed堅定立場使美國公債在紐約收盤重現政策主導的熊市趨平
overall_summary.session_narrative: 紐約收盤逆轉前一交易日前端利率回落的走勢；市場上調預期中的政策緊縮路徑，較短天期債券表現落後。Fed強調經濟活動穩健並持續警戒通膨後，這次調整主要由實質殖利率推動，而非通膨補償普遍大幅上升。原油下跌且信用市場井然有序，限制了整體波動，但長端殖利率居高不下，顯示財政供給負擔仍在。
overall_summary.summary: 成長展現韌性，加上Fed堅定維持立場，推高近期實質利率門檻，美國公債以前端跌幅最大。原油下跌、股市上漲且高收益債風險穩定，使這波走勢仍由政策持續性主導，而非普遍性的通膨或融資衝擊。

section_summaries.market_read: 收盤推高近期政策門檻，但未顯示風險資產正無序平倉。
section_summaries.next_check: 後續通膨數據必須呈現廣泛降溫，才能逆轉前端的反彈。
section_summaries.summary: Fed對成長的信心與持續警戒通膨並存，使週五美國公債收盤恢復由前端主導的熊市趨平。實質利率要求上升推動了這次調整，而原油下跌、股市走強及高收益債展現韌性，則將訊號限制在政策持續性，而非廣泛的融資壓力。

section_summaries.market_read: 限制性利率訊號由實質利率重新定價界定，而非新的長端衝擊。
section_summaries.next_check: 核心與服務通膨必須同步降溫，2年至5年期殖利率才可能回落。
section_summaries.section_title: 美國公債利率、Fed定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 較短天期美國公債領跌，殖利率曲線因而收斂；5年至10年期實質殖利率的升幅高於遠期通膨補償。此一型態顯示市場預期的政策路徑轉趨堅定，而長期實質殖利率持穩，則顯示並未出現新的長期財政或通膨衝擊。

section_summaries.market_read: 融資仍可取得，但利差補償偏薄，放大了個別發行人的脆弱性。
section_summaries.next_check: 觀察供給增加或波動升高，是否迫使交易商保留更多長存續期間風險。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 信用融資管道仍然暢通，但保護緩衝偏薄。Lendable 以逐步攤還的消費貸款 ABS 取得資金；MiniMax 將人工智慧融資分散至折價增資與零息可轉債；Shapoorji Pallonji 則透過集中型本地融資，加上殖利率 14.5% 的美元債恢復流動性。現券利差偏窄與困境案例，使融資架構仍是關鍵。

section_summaries.market_read: 全球存續期間確實獲得舒緩但並不均衡，日本投資人基礎的訊號具有雙向影響。
section_summaries.next_check: GPIF的正式回應必須說明，國內承接能否持續且不削弱美國公債需求。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 日本退休基金配置訊號推動 JGB 上漲，但 6 月生產者價格年增 7.1%，薪資也維持強勁，使政策正常化理由未消失。德國整體通膨降溫、加拿大薪資堅挺，進一步凸顯政策分歧，美債存續期間仍同時暴露於財政供給與跨境配置變化。

section_summaries.market_read: 新興市場共同融資壓力仍受控制，真正的分歧由各國內部信譽驅動。
section_summaries.next_check: 巴西能否寬鬆，仍取決於服務通膨、財政支持及8月會議。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 美元持穩與新興市場債務展現韌性，掩蓋了主權債表現的顯著分歧。巴西通膨放緩，改善了在財政限制下逐步寬鬆的條件；塞內加爾的隱藏負債、官方融資暫停及透過區域市場進行短期借款，則顯示信譽薄弱足以壓倒有利的全球環境。

section_summaries.market_read: 跨資產走勢確認實質折現率上升，而非成長或融資即將斷裂。
section_summaries.next_check: 匯率或事件波動率若大幅上升，將考驗套利交易、新興市場融資及跨境需求。
section_summaries.summary: 儘管美國公債殖利率上升，股市上漲、波動率下降及原油下跌，否定了廣泛通膨型避險行情的解讀。黃金走弱與實質利率重新定價一致，而美元持穩及高收益債展現韌性，則顯示金融情勢仍然有序，但容易受到套利交易平倉衝擊。

section_summaries.market_read: 目前論點的關鍵轉為反應函數數據、融資承接能力及日本投資人基礎。
section_summaries.next_check: 依照各項既定觸發條件，追蹤前端、國庫券標售結果及超長天期JGB。
section_summaries.summary: 美國通膨數據與Fed聽證將檢驗政策持續性是否能與通膨預期受控並存，而國庫券標售則將測試現金承接能力。日本退休基金的任何正式回應，將決定JGB上漲反映的是持久國內需求，還是僅有暫時性的口頭支持。

watch_events.market_implication: 通膨降溫將拉低2年至5年期實質殖利率；通膨維持堅挺則會印證需要進一步緊縮。
watch_events.market_trigger: 核心及服務價格全面降溫、實質所得改善，且Fed措辭顯示耐心。
watch_events.title: 美國通膨數據序列與Fed聽證
watch_events.watch_line: 追蹤2年期殖利率、短期通膨定價、美元及事件波動率。

watch_events.market_implication: 承接不佳將為前端增添現金管理溢價，並對流動性敏感型信用資產造成壓力。
watch_events.market_trigger: 投標疲弱、交易商保留更多部位，或結算前再度走低。
watch_events.title: 美國國庫券標售與現金承接
watch_events.watch_line: 觀察投標倍數、投資人參與、交易商得標比例及國庫券次級市場交易。

watch_events.market_implication: JGB獲得持久支持可緩解日本國內壓力，卻可能減少日本對美國公債存續期間的需求。
watch_events.market_trigger: 可信的國內債券配置轉向，使超長天期JGB殖利率持續下降且日圓走強。
watch_events.title: 日本公共退休基金資產配置回應
watch_events.watch_line: 觀察正式的資產配置檢討、目標區間修訂及JGB漲勢的持續性。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The July 10 Treasury close reversed the prior session’s front-end relief and restored a policy-led bear flattening. The 2-year yield returned to 4.21% and the 10-year to 4.56%, as shorter maturities underperformed even though crude fell and US equities advanced. The move renewed the central restrictive-rate interpretation carried through the week, but changed its immediate mechanism. Energy was no longer the day’s direct impulse. Investors instead raised the near-term real-rate hurdle after the Federal Reserve paired confidence in growth and productivity with an emphatic commitment to contain inflation.

The composition kept the adjustment disciplined. The 10-year real yield reached 2.32%, while inflation compensation rose only modestly. That combination indicates a higher required real return and a firmer expected policy path, not a loss of confidence in the long-run inflation anchor. The Fed’s semiannual assessment acknowledged that war, tariffs and technology costs had lifted inflation, yet also pointed to improvement in trimmed measures. With forward guidance deliberately less prescriptive, the market gave more weight to the approaching inflation data and to the risk that solid activity lets policymakers remain restrictive.

Long-duration pressure persisted without leading the daily selloff. The 30-year yield held at 5.06%, preserving the elevated funding threshold that survived the previous day’s rally. Japan provided a partial counterforce: official encouragement for pension funds to invest more at home drove a sharp JGB rally and strengthened the yen. Immediate global duration pressure eased, although the same proposal raised a longer-run question about Japanese demand for overseas securities. The US curve therefore flattened on policy risk while the international buyer-base signal moved in two directions.

Credit and cross assets argued against a generalized funding event. High-yield listed vehicles were nearly flat, equities rose and volatility fell, even as duration-heavy investment-grade returns weakened. Bank balance-sheet growth and orderly private-credit redemptions supported the availability of financing, but unusually tight cash spreads, lean dealer inventories and Aston Martin’s creditor mobilization exposed little room for error. The close combined macro resilience with selective balance-sheet caution: near-term Fed pressure strengthened, long-end compensation stayed high, and risk appetite remained intact only where leverage and refinancing demands were manageable.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasuries cheapened in descending order from the front end. The 2-year yield rose 5 basis points to 4.21%, the 5-year added 3 to 4.30%, the 10-year increased 2 to 4.56%, and the 30-year edged 1 basis point higher to 5.06%. This fully reversed Thursday’s 2-year rally and restored the benchmark and long-bond levels recorded on Wednesday. The ordering identifies a policy-path adjustment: investors demanded more compensation around the expected tightening horizon, while the incremental long-end move was smaller because substantial term and supply premia were already embedded.

Curve measures captured the bear flattening. The 2s10s spread narrowed 3 basis points to 35, and 2s30s compressed 4 to 85. The 2s5s10s butterfly fell 1 basis point to -17 as the 5-year retained some relative support against the 2-year, while the 2s10s30s butterfly declined 2 to -15 as the benchmark sector cheapened against the long bond. Friday therefore reversed the prior bull steepening without creating a fresh long-end-led fiscal selloff.

Inflation-linked securities placed real rates at the center of the move. The 5-year real yield rose 3 basis points to 2.02%, the 7-year added 2 to 2.16%, and the 10-year increased 1 to 2.32%. 20- and 30-year real yields were unchanged at 2.66% and 2.86%. The concentration of real-yield pressure in the 5- to 10-year area mirrored the nominal curve and reinforced the interpretation that the market was repricing policy persistence more strongly than distant inflation or fiscal risk.

The 10-year breakeven rose 1 basis point to 2.24%, leaving nominal and real components equally responsible for the benchmark’s 2-basis-point increase. Over 1 month, the contrast was sharper: the 10-year real yield was 12 basis points higher while the breakeven was 9 lower. Friday interrupted the latest decline in inflation compensation, but it did not overturn the broader pattern in which investors have raised the real return required for duration even as market-implied inflation receded.

Weekly changes preserved the structural burden farther out. 2-, 5- and 10-year nominal yields were each 7 basis points higher than a week earlier, while the 30-year was up 8. Over 1 month, the 2-year had risen 8 basis points and the long bond five, compared with increases of 4 and three at 5 and 10 years. The daily flattening sat inside a longer pattern of elevated yields across the curve, with neither the policy sector nor the far end able to hold Thursday’s relief.

The Federal Reserve’s semiannual Monetary Policy Report supplied the clearest same-day policy signal. It described growth as solid and productivity as strong, and it found the banking system resilient. Inflation had risen over the past year under the influence of the Iran war, tariffs and technology-goods costs, even as trimmed measures had eased. Policymakers paired that mixed diagnosis with a readiness to act forcefully if longer-run expectations came under threat, giving the rates market little basis to treat softer crude as sufficient policy relief.

Reduced forward guidance increased the value of each incoming release. Currency options had already begun to price more event risk around the next US inflation data, and money markets carried at least 25 bp Fed increase for the year. The report’s emphasis on forecast uncertainty supported a data-dependent distribution rather than a preset sequence. Friday’s front-end cheapening reflects that asymmetry: strong growth or persistent inflation can validate further restraint, while relief requires several components of inflation and demand to weaken together.

Commodity signals qualified the hawkish reading. WTI and Brent both declined on the day, and the 10-year breakeven moved only slightly. Yet refined products had not fallen in line with crude after the fragile ceasefire, as constrained refinery output and disrupted diesel supply kept crack spreads elevated. USDA’s July balance sheet added a separate food-price tail by cutting US wheat production to 1.536 billion bushels and reducing corn and global grain stocks. Neither channel produced an uncontrolled breakeven move, but both limited confidence that headline disinflation would persist.

The bill curve also shifted toward a firmer policy path. 4-week coupon-equivalent yields were unchanged at 3.69%, while 6- and 8-week yields rose 1 basis point to 3.69% and 3.72%. 13- and 17-week yields added 2 basis points to 3.80% and 3.88%, and the 26- and 52-week sectors rose 4 basis points to 3.96% and 4.06%. The progressively larger move beyond 2 months showed cash instruments repricing expected policy more than immediate funding strain.

Long-end behavior continued the week’s supply conclusion without a new deterioration in clearing capacity. The 30-year nominal yield remained above 5%, and its real yield stayed at 2.86%, even though the long bond underperformed less than shorter maturities. Current pricing confirms that investors still require substantial compensation for fiscal borrowing and competing corporate duration. At the same time, the flatter daily curve and stable long real yield do not indicate an additional shock to long-horizon inflation or demand.

The rates close consequently carried a narrower restrictive signal than the earlier oil-driven selloff. Fed communication and resilient activity raised the near-term real-rate hurdle; modestly firmer breakevens preserved an inflation tail; and falling crude prevented that tail from dominating. High long-end yields maintained the supply and term-premium theme, but the day’s relative move belonged to the front end. A durable change now requires inflation data to alter the reaction function, not merely another swing in headline energy.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Listed credit softened as Treasury yields rose, with duration explaining much of the quality split. LQD fell 0.23% to $107.46, while HYG slipped 0.05% to $79.71 and JNK declined 0.07% to $95.93. The small high-yield moves, alongside stronger equities and lower volatility, showed little broad retreat from corporate risk. Investment-grade total returns were more exposed to the rise in underlying rates, leaving cash valuation, dealer capacity and issuer-specific financing needs as the more informative measures of credit resilience.

Cash spreads entered the close with very little cushion. Corporate bonds had yielded roughly 74 basis points more than Treasuries for much of the week, near multi-decade tights. Primary dealers were net short about $4 billion of corporate bonds on average during the year through June, the first such position in data extending back to 1998. Long-dated exposure drove the stance: dealers were short roughly $13.7 billion of bonds maturing in 5 years or more, partly offset by a $9.66 billion long position in shorter maturities.

That inventory pattern has both macro and structural explanations. Higher rates and geopolitical uncertainty make long corporate bonds unattractive to warehouse when spreads are compressed. Electronic execution now represents 49% of investment-grade and 32% of high-yield trading, allowing dealers to match flows with less balance-sheet inventory. Strong pension and insurance demand can also leave little product on dealer books. The result is a liquid market in normal conditions with less visible intermediation capacity if yields or spreads move abruptly.

European valuations showed the same tension between strong fundamentals and limited compensation. Investment-grade spreads were near their tightest since the global financial crisis after retracing the widening that accompanied the Iran war. RBC Wealth Management viewed euro-denominated debt as particularly expensive and saw better relative value in dollar and sterling markets, while still acknowledging healthy corporate balance sheets. The concern was valuation and duration exposure, not an immediate deterioration in borrower quality.

Lendable’s £500 million personal-loan securitization showed where investors were still being paid for shorter, higher-yielding exposure. The collateral came from a platform that had originated more than £10 billion of consumer credit and reported 2025 revenue of £446 million, up 90%, with £114 million of operating profit. The transaction broadened funding for UK, US and Mexican lending while transferring consumer-performance risk into asset-backed securities. Its appeal contrasted with long investment-grade cash bonds: shorter amortizing assets offered spread and principal repayment, though unsecured household credit remains sensitive to employment and refinancing conditions.

MiniMax used a hybrid route for AI funding. The Chinese model developer sought HK$9.5 billion through 35.6 million new shares at HK$268, a 9.9% discount, alongside HK$6.5 billion of zero-coupon convertible bonds due 2027 with a 25% conversion premium. The package could raise close to $2 billion while sharing financing risk between immediate equity dilution and future conversion. It also extended the AI capital-demand theme beyond US hyperscaler bonds: when valuations permit, equity and convertibles can reduce cash interest, but a nearly 9.8% share-price decline showed the dilution and execution cost.

Commercial-bank data supported funding availability but revealed slower business-credit momentum. June bank credit grew at a 4.7% annualized rate, and loans and leases rose 5.2%. Commercial-and-industrial lending increased 3.7%, down sharply from 10.9% in May and 15.9% in April. Deposits expanded 5.3% as other deposits grew 7.6% and large time deposits contracted 10%. The figures describe an operating lending channel whose marginal impulse to businesses was cooling.

The H.8 release arrived at 4:15 p.m. ET, making it relevant to the final portion of the close without explaining the earlier rates move. For the week through July 1, bank credit stood at $19.6151 trillion, C&I loans at $2.8781 trillion and deposits at $19.4345 trillion, each slightly below the preceding week’s level. The monthly expansion and weekly softness fit a financing environment that remains functional but offers less acceleration to leveraged corporate activity.

The Fed’s financial-stability assessment drew a similar boundary. Aggregate household and business debt relative to GDP was the lowest since the early 2000s, bank capital remained high and uninsured deposits had declined since early 2023. Hedge-fund leverage, however, remained near record highs and concentrated among a small group. Some private-credit vehicles faced increased redemption requests, generally met up to 5% of net asset value while markets continued to operate normally. Liquidity was available, but leverage and redemption terms remained potential amplifiers.

Aston Martin illustrated how that benign aggregate backdrop can coexist with acute capital-structure risk. Arini Capital Management, BlackRock and Sculptor led a group holding more than half of the carmaker’s senior secured notes and offered fresh financing while seeking to prevent transactions that could subordinate existing claims. Q1 pretax losses were £65.5 million, net debt reached £1.46 billion, and $1.85 billion equivalent of bonds mature in 2029. The cooperation agreement shifted the debate from broad market access to priority, documentation and recovery value.

Shapoorji Pallonji occupied the refinancing space between open markets and distress. The Indian group launched a 255 billion-rupee, or $2.7 billion, plan after twice extending debt at Goswami Infratech. About 150 billion rupees of local bonds were directed to investors including Farallon, Davidson Kempner and Cerberus, while a separate $650 million 3-year dollar bond cleared at 14.5%; Deutsche Bank could invest about $400 million. The package restored near-term liquidity, but the double-digit dollar yield and reliance on concentrated institutional capital showed how expensive access remained while delays in monetizing the group’s 18.4% Tata Sons stake complicated deleveraging.

Credit therefore stayed open but thinly protected. Investor demand and technology-enabled trading supported secondary liquidity, banks continued to expand credit, and private markets handled higher redemptions. Tight spreads reduced compensation for macro surprises, dealer shorts concentrated risk in longer maturities, and distressed borrowers required creditor coordination before maturities became immediate. Friday’s mild fund losses were compatible with functioning markets, yet the combination of high base yields and narrow spreads leaves financing outcomes increasingly dependent on structure and issuer cash generation.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

Japan delivered the strongest developed-market counterforce to the US selloff. Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama urged pension funds, including the Government Pension Investment Fund, to invest more in domestic financial assets. The comments drove the 20-year JGB yield down 12 basis points to 3.745% and the 10-year yield down 11.5 basis points to 2.76%, while the yen gained as much as 0.7%. That rally reduced immediate global duration pressure during Asian trading and showed how buyer-base policy can overpower the week’s inflation concerns for a session.

The cross-border implication was less uniformly supportive. GPIF manages ¥293.6 trillion and invests about half its assets overseas; Japan also holds roughly $1.2 trillion of US Treasuries. A lasting shift toward domestic bonds and equities could stabilize the steep JGB curve and reduce structural yen selling, yet it could also lower the pool allocated to foreign duration. The immediate JGB rally helped global bonds, whereas a completed reallocation could make Treasury demand more sensitive to relative yields and currency hedging costs.

Implementation remains uncertain. GPIF is overseen by the labor ministry, follows a 5-year strategic allocation process and kept equal 25% shares in domestic bonds, domestic equities, foreign bonds and foreign equities at its March 2025 review. Katayama’s remarks therefore changed expectations before changing mandates. The rally offered relief from fiscal and BOJ-normalization pressure, but it did not resolve whether public pensions will absorb more domestic issuance or whether the government was using verbal guidance to ease stress in the yen and long JGBs.

Japanese producer prices strengthened the inflation side of that allocation debate. The BOJ’s corporate-goods index rose 7.1% from a year earlier in June, the fastest pace since early 2023, and increased 0.4% from May after the prior month was revised higher. Together with wage settlements above 5% for a 3rd year, the result indicated that firms were passing more input costs through rather than absorbing them entirely in margins. Pension demand can stabilize JGB duration, but it does not remove the policy case for normalization when corporate pricing, credit growth and wages are all firm.

German inflation supplied a two-sided European duration signal. June CPI was confirmed at 2.3% year over year and fell 0.3% from May, down from a 2.6% annual pace in May. HICP rose 2.4% over the year and fell 0.2% on the month. Energy prices declined 3.0% monthly, including sharp falls in motor fuel and heating oil, giving Bunds a near-term disinflation channel. Core inflation held at 2.5% and services rose 3.1%, preserving an underlying-price constraint for the ECB.

Canada’s labor report leaned more firmly against near-term duration relief, though it was not uniformly strong. Employment rose 18,000 after an 88,000 May gain, the employment rate increased to 60.8%, and unemployment eased to 6.5%. Average hourly wages accelerated to 3.3% year over year from 3.0%. A 17,000 decline in manufacturing employment exposed sectoral weakness, but firmer wages and lower unemployment kept the Bank of Canada’s inflation-growth tradeoff restrictive.

ECB Governing Council member Emmanuel Moulin added a structural reason for caution. He argued that artificial intelligence can raise inflation in the near term through capital spending and lower it later through productivity, increasing volatility around the price path without yet changing current policy. The message echoed the Fed’s own treatment of AI as both a productivity source and a driver of technology-goods demand. Across jurisdictions, large investment programs are supporting activity while complicating the timing of disinflation.

Global duration ended the day with dispersion, not a synchronized selloff. Japan’s prospective domestic-buyer support lowered JGB yields sharply, German energy disinflation softened the headline signal, and Canadian wages kept policy restraint credible. For Treasuries, those forces split between immediate rate relief and longer-run competition for capital. Friday’s US bear flattening could proceed even as JGBs rallied because the dominant US catalyst was the Fed path, while the persistent 30-year yield reflected unresolved fiscal supply and cross-border allocation risk.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market dollar debt held up despite higher Treasury yields. EMB rose 0.03% to $95.99, while the US Dollar Index was almost unchanged, gaining 0.03% to 100.97. The combination spared hard-currency borrowers an additional dollar shock even as US real yields increased. Aggregate performance therefore looked stable, but sovereign outcomes remained divided between countries gaining room to ease and those confronting impaired market access.

Brazil received the clearest local-duration relief. Annual inflation slowed to 4.64% in June, below every estimate in a survey whose median was 4.80%, and monthly inflation eased to 0.16%, the slowest since October. Food and beverages fell 0.24%, offsetting a 0.63% increase in housing. The downside surprise followed weak May job creation and strengthened the case for another quarter-point reduction at the August 5 policy meeting after three consecutive cuts.

Fiscal policy limits the size of that relief. The Selic rate stands at 14.25%, and central-bank projections still placed end-2026 inflation above the upper boundary of the target range. Household debt-renegotiation support, subsidized credit and a temporary suspension of some fuel taxes add demand as the election approaches. Softer current inflation can extend gradual easing, but persistent services, housing or fiscal stimulus would keep real rates high and constrain the repricing of Brazilian local debt.

Senegal occupied the opposite end of the debt-management spectrum. An audit uncovered about $7 billion of previously unreported loans, and subsequent reviews lifted estimated public debt above 130% of GDP from an earlier 74%. The IMF suspended a $1.8 billion program, ratings moved deeper into junk and the sovereign lost effective access to the eurobond market. Its international bonds traded at an average spread exceeding 15 percentage points over Treasuries, reflecting expectations that maturity extension or restructuring may become unavoidable.

The financing strain reaches beyond sovereign bondholders. Senegal faces about 5.5 trillion CFA francs of debt repayments this year against roughly 5.4 trillion francs of expected tax revenue. Reliance on the regional West African market has increased banks’ exposure to short-dated government bills, transmitting sovereign risk into local balance sheets. Projects financed in euros and dollars generate revenue in CFA francs, creating a currency mismatch that domestic cash flows cannot easily close. Official credibility and foreign-exchange generation now matter more than global risk appetite.

EM conditions were consequently calm at the index level and sharply differentiated underneath. A stable dollar and resilient listed debt reduced the common refinancing shock. Brazil’s inflation surprise improved the probability of measured local easing, subject to fiscal limits. Senegal’s hidden liabilities, suspended official financing and short-term regional borrowing showed how weak disclosure can shut a current sovereign out of hard-currency markets. The rise in US real yields remained a hurdle, but domestic policy credibility determined whether it was marginal pressure or an existential constraint.

## Cross asset

US equities treated Friday’s macro mix as compatible with continued growth. The S&P 500 gained 0.42% to 7,575.39, the Nasdaq 100 rose 0.33% to 29,825.11, and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index added 0.06% to 12,967.16. The VIX fell 5.11% to 15.03. Stronger stocks and lower volatility alongside higher Treasury yields support a real-rate and policy interpretation of the bond selloff, with little sign that investors were seeking protection from an immediate growth break.

Energy moved in the opposite direction from rates. WTI declined 0.93% to $71.41 and Brent fell 0.38% to $76.01. Both remained higher over 1 week, by 3.96% and 5.86%, while still showing steep 1-month losses. The daily decline eased the direct headline-inflation impulse that had driven earlier sessions. Elevated refined-fuel margins and renewed geopolitical friction kept the transmission risk alive, explaining why breakevens firmed slightly even as crude itself retreated.

The dollar was nearly unchanged at 100.97, leaving the rise in US yields without a forceful currency confirmation. Gold fell 0.64% to $4,104.10 despite geopolitical uncertainty. Higher real yields increased the opportunity cost of holding bullion, and the absence of a strong dollar move kept the decline from becoming a broad haven liquidation. Gold’s weakness aligned more closely with the real-rate repricing than with the day’s benign equity tone.

Low foreign-exchange volatility remained an important support for risk taking, but hedging costs had begun to rise around the coming US inflation release. Wide global rate gaps and calm currencies had helped G10 carry strategies return about 8% during the year. Reduced Fed guidance makes that stability more dependent on each data release. A sharp volatility pickup could force leveraged carry positions to unwind, transmitting a US policy surprise through the dollar, EM funding costs and demand for cross-border duration.

The cross-asset close therefore complicated the bond move without contradicting it. Falling oil, rising equities and lower volatility rejected a broad inflationary risk-off episode. Gold weakness and the front-end-led Treasury selloff pointed to a higher real discount rate, while a flat dollar and resilient high yield showed that financial conditions had not tightened disorderly. Markets accepted stronger policy restraint alongside continued growth, leaving the vulnerable point in any future shock at the intersection of leveraged carry, tight credit spreads and high real yields.

## Forward Market Significance

June CPI and real earnings at 8:30 a.m. ET on July 14 begin the decisive reaction-function sequence, followed by Chair Kevin Warsh’s House testimony at 10:00 a.m., June PPI at 8:30 a.m. on July 15 and his Senate appearance at 10:00 a.m. A broad cooling in core and services prices, improving real earnings and limited producer-price pass-through, accompanied by an acknowledgment that policy can remain patient, would challenge Friday’s front-end-led reversal and pull 2- to 5-year real yields lower. Firm underlying inflation, weak real-income growth or an explicit emphasis on further restraint would confirm that falling crude has not reduced policy urgency. The 2-year yield, short-dated inflation compensation, the dollar and event volatility are the most sensitive channels, because the current thesis depends on a restrictive Fed path coexisting with anchored longer-run expectations.

At 11:30 a.m. ET on July 13, Treasury offers $92 billion of 13-week bills and $79 billion of 26-week bills, followed at the same time on July 14 by a $95 billion 42-day sale; all three settle July 16. Strong coverage, broad direct and indirect participation, limited dealer awards and stable secondary bill trading would resolve part of the concern that large cash absorption could disturb otherwise healthy funding conditions. Weak bidding, heavier dealer retention or renewed cheapening into settlement would reopen that risk, particularly after outer bill yields rose more than the shortest maturities on Friday. The bill curve, secured funding conditions and bank-liquidity-sensitive credit are the transmission channels. Orderly absorption would confirm that the bear flattening reflected policy expectations, whereas poor demand would add a cash-management premium to the front end.

A formal response from GPIF or Japan’s labor ministry to the call for greater domestic investment has no fixed date, but any initiation of an allocation review, change in target bands or guidance to other public pensions would provide the observable trigger. A credible shift toward domestic bonds that sustains lower super-long JGB yields and a firmer yen would confirm that Japan can stabilize its own curve through the buyer base, while simultaneously raising the question of reduced Japanese demand for Treasuries. A denial, procedural delay or rapid reversal of Friday’s JGB rally would challenge the immediate relief and reopen fiscal and BOJ-normalization pressure. Super-long JGBs, the yen, currency-hedged Treasury returns and the US long end would reveal whether the policy signal creates durable domestic absorption or only a temporary verbal-intervention effect.

section_summaries.market_read: The close raised the near-term policy hurdle without signaling a disorderly risk unwind.
section_summaries.next_check: Incoming inflation data must show broad cooling to unwind the front-end reversal.
section_summaries.summary: Friday’s Treasury close restored a front-end-led bear flattening as Fed confidence in growth met continued inflation vigilance. Higher real-rate demands drove the adjustment, while falling crude, stronger equities and resilient high yield limited the signal to policy persistence rather than broad funding stress.

section_summaries.market_read: Real-rate repricing, not a fresh long-end shock, defined the restrictive rates signal.
section_summaries.next_check: Core and services inflation must cool together before 2- to 5-year yields can retrace.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: Shorter Treasury maturities led the selloff, compressing the curve as 5- to 10-year real yields rose more than distant inflation compensation. The pattern identified a firmer expected policy path, while the stable long real yield showed no new long-horizon fiscal or inflation shock.

section_summaries.market_read: Financing stayed available, but thin spread compensation amplified issuer-specific fragility.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether heavier supply or volatility forces dealers to retain more long-duration risk.
section_summaries.summary: Credit remained open but thinly protected. Lendable found demand for amortizing consumer ABS, MiniMax split AI funding between discounted equity and zero-coupon convertibles, and Shapoorji Pallonji restored liquidity through concentrated local funding plus a 14.5% dollar bond; tight cash spreads and distressed cases kept structure decisive.

section_summaries.market_read: Global duration relief was real but uneven, with Japan’s buyer-base signal cutting both ways.
section_summaries.next_check: A formal GPIF response must show whether domestic absorption can persist without weakening Treasury demand.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: Japan’s pension-allocation signal rallied JGBs, but June producer prices rose 7.1% year over year and wages remained firm, preserving the normalization case. German headline disinflation and Canadian wage strength reinforced policy dispersion, leaving Treasury duration exposed to fiscal supply and cross-border allocation shifts.

section_summaries.market_read: Common EM funding stress stayed contained, while domestic credibility drove the real dispersion.
section_summaries.next_check: Brazilian easing remains conditional on services inflation, fiscal support and the August meeting.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: A stable dollar and resilient EM debt masked sharp sovereign divergence. Brazil’s softer inflation improved the case for gradual easing within fiscal limits, while Senegal’s hidden liabilities, suspended official financing and short-dated regional borrowing showed how weak credibility can overwhelm benign global conditions.

section_summaries.market_read: Cross assets confirmed a higher real discount rate, not an immediate growth or funding break.
section_summaries.next_check: A jump in currency or event volatility would test carry, EM funding and cross-border demand.
section_summaries.summary: Rising equities, lower volatility and falling crude rejected a broad inflationary risk-off episode despite higher Treasury yields. Gold weakness aligned with the real-rate repricing, while a steady dollar and resilient high yield showed financial conditions remained orderly but vulnerable to a carry unwind.

section_summaries.market_read: The thesis now turns on reaction-function data, funding absorption and Japan’s buyer base.
section_summaries.next_check: Track the front end, bill clearing and super-long JGBs against each stated trigger.
section_summaries.summary: US inflation releases and Fed testimony will test whether policy persistence can coexist with anchored expectations, while bill auctions probe cash absorption. Any formal Japanese pension response will determine whether the JGB rally reflects durable domestic demand or only temporary verbal support.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

bullets: 10Y TIPS real yield 2.32% (+1 bp) | 10Y breakeven inflation 2.24% (+1 bp)
UST 2s10s curve 35 bp (-3 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 85 bp (-4 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Fed resolve restores a policy-led Treasury bear flattening at the NY close
overall_summary.session_narrative: The NY close reversed the prior session’s front-end relief, with shorter maturities underperforming as the market raised the expected path of policy restraint. Real yields, rather than a broad jump in inflation compensation, carried the adjustment after the Fed emphasized solid activity and vigilance on inflation. Falling crude and orderly credit limited the move, while elevated long-end yields preserved the fiscal-supply burden.
overall_summary.summary: Treasuries cheapened most at the front end as resilient growth and firm Fed resolve lifted the near-term real-rate hurdle. Falling crude, rising equities and steady high-yield risk kept the move anchored in policy persistence rather than a generalized inflation or funding shock.

watch_events.market_implication: Cooling would pull 2- to 5-year real yields lower; firmness would validate further restraint.
watch_events.market_trigger: Broad cooling across core and services prices with improving real earnings and patient Fed language.
watch_events.title: US inflation sequence and Fed testimony
watch_events.watch_line: Follow the 2-year yield, short-dated inflation pricing, dollar and event volatility.

watch_events.market_implication: Poor absorption would add a cash-management premium to the front end and pressure liquidity-sensitive credit.
watch_events.market_trigger: Weak bidding, heavier dealer retention or renewed cheapening into settlement.
watch_events.title: Treasury bill auctions and cash absorption
watch_events.watch_line: Watch coverage, investor participation, dealer awards and secondary bill trading.

watch_events.market_implication: Durable JGB support could ease local stress yet reduce Japanese demand for Treasury duration.
watch_events.market_trigger: A credible domestic-bond shift that sustains lower super-long JGB yields and a firmer yen.
watch_events.title: Japan public-pension allocation response
watch_events.watch_line: Watch for a formal allocation review, revised target bands and persistence of the JGB rally.
